mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during months with positive PAYEMS changes compared to negative changes (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, ultra-accommodative monetary policy suppresses short-term discount rates and incentivizes risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following trigger events are negative on average or exhibit a Sharpe ratio below zero over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) generate a non-linear demand impulse that exceeds the sum of individual effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and HOUST does not produce a higher mean 20-day forward return than either single-series signal alone (linear decomposition test p < 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions where unemployment is already below its natural rate, further month-over-month declines in UNRATE signal an overheating labor market that accelerates wage-push inflation fears and monetary tightening, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE declines below 4.5 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing output and corporate earnings compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending momentum that translates into near-term revenue beats and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds a 3 percent annualized threshold, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers discount-rate repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower after CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent annualized change compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production (INDPRO) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) confirms a non-inflationary supply response to labor demand, reducing stagflation risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE changes does not exhibit a statistically significant positive coefficient (p < 0.05) in predicting 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
